titlestmt.author: Processed by Arts Special Collections Staff, pre-1999; machine-readable finding aid created by Caroline Cubé after initial encoding by Julie Graham.

address.addressline: Room A1713, Charles E. Young Research Library

publicationstmt.date: ©2014 The Regents of the University of California. All rights reserved.

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using the Archivists' Toolkit 2014-10-17T09:24-0700. Supplementary encoding and revision by Caroline Cubé using Notetab Pro.

did.abstract: Eugene Rodney was best known as producer of the television program, Father Knows Best. The bulk of the collection includes scripts and production information for the radio and television series Father Knows Best, and the short lived television series Window on Main Street, all featuring Robert Young.
did.physloc: Stored off-site at SRLF. Advance notice is required for access to the collection. Please contact UCLA Library Special Collections for paging information.

accessrestrict.head: Restrictions on Access
accessrestrict.p: Open for research. STORED OFF-SITE AT SRLF. Advance notice is required for access to the collection. Please contact UCLA Library Special Collections for paging information.

userestrict.head: Restrictions on Use and Reproduction
userestrict.p: Property rights to the physical object belong to the UC Regents. Literary rights, including copyright, are retained by the creators and their heirs. It is the responsibility of the researcher to determine who holds the copyright and pursue the copyright owner or his or her heir for permission to publish where The UC Regents do not hold the copyright.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: [Identification of item], Eugene Rodney papers (Collection PASC 163). UCLA Library Special Collections, Charles E. Young Research Library, UCLA.

bioghist.head: Biography
bioghist.p: Eugene B. Rodney was originally a publicist for East Coast theater owners and stage producers. He moved west when a theater chain he was managing was sold. In Hollywood he became an associate producer at Reliance Pictures and was involved in the filming of The Last of the Mohicans, The Count of Monte Cristo and Red Salute with Robert Young. Rodney went on to become the Vice-President of Production for Robert Young's production company, Cavalier Productions, Inc., and helped conceptualize and then produce the television version of Father Knows Best. Rodney died in November, 1985.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: The collection consists of materials related to the career of television producer and director Eugene Rodney. The bulk of the collection comprises files for the radio and television versions of Father Knows Best and short lived television series, Window On Main Street. Father Knows Best radio scripts represent 1949-1954; the television files represent ca. 1955-ca. 1960 and include scripts and production material. Window on Main Street scripts and production information represent thirty-plus episodes. Additionally, there is a very small amount of Rodney's personal business files, a small number of photographs of Father Knows Best cast members, and a small number of screenplays for projects in which Rodney was involved in some capacity.

arrangement.head: Organization and Arrangement
arrangement.p:
Arranged in the following series:
Radio Projects
Telelvision Projects
Personal Business Papers
Unidentified Projects

odd.head: UCLA Catalog Record ID
odd.p: UCLA Catalog Record ID: 2723137

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: The Radio Project series documents Rodney’s involvement with the radio version of Father Knows Best, which started as a radio sitcom in 1949 on NBC.

odd.p: Numbers 6 through 10.
1949, number 2
1949, numbers 11 through 19.
1949, number 19.
1950, numbers 1 through 10.
1950, numbers 21 through 28.
1950, numbers 29 through 36.
1950, numbers 37 through 46.
1950, number 37.
1951, numbers 21 through 25.
1951, numbers 41 through 44.
1951, numbers 1 through 5.
1951, numbers 16 through 20.
1951, numbers 31 through 35.
1951, numbers 36 through 40.
1951, numbers 11 through 15.
1952, numbers A1 through A6.
1952, numbers A1 through A8.
1952, numbers A7, A8.
1952, numbers B9 through B12.
1952, numbers B13 through B17.
1953, numbers 1 through 5.
1953, numbers 6 through 10.
1953, numbers 11 through 15.
1953, numbers 16 through 20.
1953, numbers 16, 17, 18 (Summer Series).
1953, numbers C27 through C30.
1953, numbers C18 through C25.
1953, numbers C23 through C30.
1953, numbers C18 through C22.
1953, numbers D31 through D39.
1954, numbers 22 through 26.
Assorted undated outlines, drafts, treatments, and other material regarding story ideas for half-hour television shows.
First and second drafts by Dorothy Cooper, dated January 3, 1960 and January 12, 1960. Possibly for new television series.
Set of two polished marble and brass placques awarded to Father Knows Best for Best Situation Comedy Series from 1958 to 1959.
Three certificates awarded to Father Knows Best from 1958-1959 as Best Situation Comedy Series. Robert Young and Jane Wyatt are also noted as Outstanding Actor and Actress. Signed by the director.
Various contracts, agreements, financial statements, screenwriter correspondence, company information, and other material.
Income schedules and budget statements from Bisgeier, Breslauer & Co. for Eugene Rodney.
Final draft by Barbara Hammer and Dorothy Cooper, dated January 13, 1956. With various drafts and re-writes.

did.unittitle: WINDOW ON MAIN STREET title cards

odd.p: Set of two black and white title cards for the television series.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: Included are Father Knows Best cast member stills, insurance company information, business and personal correspondence, and a small amount of correspondence and clippings regarding the Myasthenia Gravis Foundation. There is no specific arrangement to the series.

odd.p: Box contains 42 various Father Knows Best cast member stills, insurance company information, Robert Young file, correspondence and other material.
Box contains Rodney Bel Air Property information, personal correspondence and business matters, lear jet contracts, Myasthenia Gravis Foundation correspondence, and other material.
Box contains publishing correspondence, legal matters, insurance contracts, Goldstone agreements, and other miscellaneous folders.
Correspondence and clippings, etc. from Robert Young regarding the Myasthenia Gravis Foundation.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: The series includes files which are not identified with known projects and in which Rodney was involved in some capacity, and which may or may not have been produced. Mostly includes drafts of screenplays with occasional production information for projects such as The Fan Letter , The Happy Scoundrel , and 1600 Pennsylvania Avenue , among others. The series is arranged alphabetically by project title.

odd.p: Screenplay by Berne Geler (based on story by Sidney Biddell and Frederic Frank), dated October 29, 1951. Includes various drafts and re-writes.
Various scripts and articles, including revised first draft step-outlines, treatments with notes, and artist renditions the White House used as research for the screenplay.
Screenplay by Sidney Biddell and Fredric Frank, dated November 1951. With various drafts, basic material and re-writes.
Incomplete draft revision, dated December 4, 1951.
Producer's Copy of screenplay by Roswell Rogers, no date.
Various letters, handwritten notes, and story outlines for screenplay to star Robert Young.
Production information: shooting schedule, story outlines, stills used for scene research, and other material.
Several screenplay drafts by Roswell Rogers, dated September 23, 1960 and November 21, 1960. Also includes detailed set designs and blueprint ideas.
Working screenplay by Ed James, dated November 21, 1952.
Lobby card; featuring Debi Doyle, no date.
First final draft by Eugene B. Rodney, dated September 26, 1947.

did.unittitle: PASSING OF THE THIRD FLOOR BACK (THE)

odd.p: Written in three acts by Jerome K. Jerome, no date.
First draft Screenplay by Susan Ertz, no date.
Third draft by A.E. Hotchner, dated 1963.
Revised draft "A" by A.E. Hotchner, dated 1963.
Screenplay by Stanley Roberts, no date.
